TextENGNew: How many days had it been since then? Today, once again, Satoko's seat sat empty.
Our gazes crossed as we sat in our seats around Satoko's empty one.
Even so, as if by some unspoken agreement, we all put on the pretense of normalcy.
It's nothing serious, right? Surely it's just a cold.
Tomorrow, Satoko would appear before us again with that carefree, innocent smile of hers. She had to.
changeReason: New adds 'She had to' (はずだ = should/must), conveying the narrator's desperation/insistence not present in old.

TextENGNew: And yet, I couldn't stop a heavy, pitch-black sediment of anxiety from pooling in the depths of my heart.
changeReason: Old omits 重くて、どす黒い (heavy, pitch-black) descriptors of the anxiety; new translates them, significantly changing the intensity of the imagery.

TextENGNew: ...Something was happening to Satoko. Of that, there could be little doubt.
If that was the case, then just sitting here in silence with our hands folded wasn't going to accomplish anything, was it?
Everyone else was probably thinking the same thing. Their eyes kept drifting to Satoko's empty seat... and sighs escaped their lips with conspicuous frequency.
The suffocating atmosphere in the room... I just couldn't bear it any longer.
``Is Satoko absent again today...?``
Rika-chan bit her lip and, with her gaze cast downward, gave a small nod. ...It was a response she had already repeated many times over.
``Rika-chan, if you know something, please tell me.@k@vS18/01/160100003. What's happening with Satoko right now?@k@vS18/01/160100004. She's been absent so many days -- what on earth is going on?``
``......There's nothing... that can be done anymore...``
changeReason: Old translates as 'can't be helped' (shikata ga nai nuance); JPN どうしようもない means 'there's nothing that can be done' -- a statement of helplessness, not resignation. New captures hopelessness better.

TextENGNew: Nothing... that can be done...?
Those words denied everything -- past, present, and future alike.
More than anything, the fact that those words came from the mouth of Rika-chan -- the person closest to Satoko in all the world -- left me... honestly stunned.
``Nothing can be done...?@k@vS18/01/160100006. What do you mean by that?``
Without answering my question, Rika-chan simply remained silent, her head bowed.
Rika-chan had to be suffering the most. I understood that much... at least intellectually.
But in my head, far more blood than I could handle was swirling around at a furious pace, and calm judgment was no longer an option.

``Tell me the reason!@k@vS18/01/160100008. Just giving me the conclusion isn't enough -- I can't accept that.@k@vS18/01/160100009. Rika-chan, you know something, don't you?@k@vS18/01/160100010. Come on!``
changeReason: Old paraphrases loosely; new more faithfully translates 訳 as 'reason' and 結果だけ as 'just the conclusion.'

TextENGNew: Perhaps because my words had grown too harsh, Rika-chan flinched, her shoulders drawing up as though in fright.
Still, she refused to meet my eyes. She just wore an expression of resignation... her lips pressed tightly together.
Her body had gone limp and felt terribly light, and with a pang that squeezed my chest, I snapped back to my senses.
changeReason: Old fabricates 'dialed back the pressure'; Japanese describes Rika going limp and feeling light.

TextENGNew: ``I'm sorry... Rika-chan.@k@vS18/01/160100014. I got too worked up...``
I apologized honestly. It wasn't just me -- Rika-chan was going through the same thing.
...Thinking of it that way, I couldn't help but feel a fresh wave of guilt and shame.
``Keiichi is not in the wrong.@k@vS18/05/160500005. There is no need to apologize.``
Rika-chan shook her head vigorously, then looked down with a shadowed gaze.
Once again, a deep silence fell over the classroom.
Someone who should have been here... was not. Everyone was shaken by Satoko's sudden, inexplicable absence.
I was surprised even at myself... to realize just how deeply Satoko's absence had shaken and unsettled my heart.
And it forced me to confront something I didn't want to face.
changeReason: Old says 'reminded me'; Japanese 否応なしに気付かされる means 'forced to realize.' New captures the involuntary nature.

TextENGNew: My own helplessness... my own insignificance... the fact that I was nothing more than an ordinary student, just a powerless kid with no ability to change anything...
``?! Could it be----``
The silence was suddenly broken. As if something had just occurred to her, Rena's eyes flew wide open.
``...Could it be that Satoko-chan's uncle... has come back...?``
Her uncle...?
At that one unexpected remark, Rika-chan's shoulders jolted as if struck, and she sank further into herself.
changeReason: Old says shoulders 'began to tremble'; Japanese 跳ね上げる means a sudden jolt, not ongoing trembling.

TextENGNew: Even so, Rena moved closer and crouched down, peering up into her face.
I could see Rika-chan tense up defensively at the almost cornering gesture.
changeReason: Old completely misreads the scene: says gesture 'alleviates tension' but Japanese describes cornering/pressuring and Rika becoming defensive.

TextENGNew: ``Has Satoko-chan's uncle come back? Has he?``
But Rika-chan remained silent, neither confirming nor denying.
...Seeing that reaction, Rena barely managed to maintain a smile, but the look in her eyes grew increasingly dangerous.
changeReason: Old says 'precarious light'; JPN 剣呑な (dangerous/threatening) and ますます (increasingly). New's 'increasingly dangerous' is more accurate than 'precarious.'

TextENGNew: ``Rena, what are you talking about?@k@vS18/01/160100016. What's this about her uncle?``
Rena silently glanced toward Rika-chan. But Rika-chan still said nothing.
Seeing that response, Rena lowered her eyes with a helpless expression and quietly began to speak.

``It happened three years ago.@k@vS18/02/160200005. After Satoko-chan's parents died in an accident, her aunt and uncle became the guardians for her and her brother.@k@vS18/02/160200006. And then, they moved right into Satoko-chan's house...``
changeReason: Old says guardians for 'her'; new says 'for her and her brother' matching JPN 二人の保護者 (guardians of the two [children]).

TextENGNew: What followed was a harrowing story... far beyond anything I could have imagined.
The aunt and uncle, upon becoming guardians, had essentially seized all of the family's assets for themselves.
changeReason: Old says 'took over her house'; JPN says 全財産を半ば奪い取る (essentially seized all assets). New captures the scope (all assets, not just the house) and the forceful nature.

TextENGNew: Satoko and Satoshi were crammed into a tiny room and forced to endure wretched living conditions.
The aunt and uncle had always been on bad terms, their quarrels unceasing.
And as if to vent their frustrations, they took their anger out on Satoko again and again.
What exactly they did to her... Rena fell silent on that point, but it wasn't hard to imagine it had been terrible.
And so Satoko was driven into a corner, both physically and emotionally...
But then... during last year's Watanagashi, the aunt was murdered, and Satoshi went missing...
Immediately afterward, the uncle fled to the city, terrified of Oyashiro-sama's curse.
changeReason: Old says 'fled the town'; JPN says 町へ逃げ込んだ (fled TO the city/town). The uncle fled Hinamizawa to a city, not from a town.

TextENGNew: Left completely alone in the world, Satoko had been living a quiet life with Rika-chan ever since, finding solace in their peaceful days together as she slowly healed her wounds.
changeReason: Old says 'feeling lonely' but 天涯孤独 means 'alone in the world' (no living relatives) -- about being orphaned, not just loneliness.

TextENGNew: However...
``...He just waltzed right back...``

``...That man... the man called Teppei Houjou is truly violent, crude, and despicable.@k@vS18/02/160200009. He grovels before the strong and takes out his frustrations on the weak.@k@vS18/02/160200010. ......Just remembering him makes me want to throw up.``
changeReason: Old condenses 乱暴でガサツでひどい (violent, crude, terrible) into 'savage brute'; new preserves all three JPN descriptors. Also old's 'Sucking up to the powerful, while terrorizing the feeble' differs from new's more literal 'grovels before the strong and takes out his frustrations on the weak.'

TextENGNew: Such harsh words were unlike Rena. ...That alone told me how utterly irredeemable Satoko's uncle must be.
``I'm sure he's already working Satoko-chan to the bone again without any remorse... and even...!``
changeReason: Old says 'taking advantage'; Japanese こき使う means 'to work someone hard.' Also 懲りずに captured in new but not old.

TextENGNew: ``What you just told me... is it really true...?``
``...Then we have to save her, now!@k@vS18/01/160100019. If things stay like this, Satoko will...!``
changeReason: Old finishes the thought ('We can't let this happen'); new leaves it unfinished ('Satoko will...!') matching the JPN interrupted sentence 沙都子は……！ which trails off ominously.

TextENGNew: ``I know.@k@vS18/02/160200014. But it's not that simple, is it?@k@vS18/02/160200015. Mii-chan?``
Saying that, Rena turned to look behind her, a faint trace of revulsion crossing her face.
Standing there was Mion, who had been keeping silent this entire time. Caught off guard by the sudden attention, she averted her eyes with a flustered look.
``It's pointless to even try...@k@vS18/02/160200017. That's what you think, isn't it?@k@vS18/02/160200018. Mii-chan.``
``...I wouldn't go that far, but the situation isn't exactly straightforward...``
``The situation isn't straightforward``...? I didn't understand what she meant and was about to ask, but Rena beat me to it with an explanation.
``The same thing happened last year.@k@vS18/02/160200019_1. We all went to the child consultation center in Okinomiya and told them that Satoko-chan's aunt and uncle were mistreating her.``
``But... Satoko-chan herself insisted that nothing was being done to her, so the whole thing just fizzled out...@k@vS18/02/160200022. And after that, the harassment from those two only got worse...``
changeReason: Old confuses who 'the two' refers to: 二人の嫌がらせ means harassment FROM those two [aunt/uncle], not suffered by them.

TextENGNew: A past failure of devastating cruelty. ...If that was what had happened, it was no wonder they were reluctant to try again.
changeReason: Old interprets as 'intervention resulted in more cruelty'; JPN 残酷なまでの、過去の失敗事例 means 'a past failure case, cruel to the point of devastation.' New is more literal.

TextENGNew: Then what? ...What else could we possibly do?!
``It doesn't matter how much we struggle, we can't change a thing...@k@vS18/03/160300003. As sad as that is...``
``...But Satoshi-kun tried to change things.``
At those words from Rena, silence once again filled the entire classroom.
Every pair of eyes converged on her at once. They held their breath, staring at her as if she had committed some unspeakable transgression...
``That's why Satoshi-kun...``
What cut Rena off was Mion, slamming her clenched fist down hard on the desk.
``--I told you before to stop bringing that up...!``
Rena and Mion, who should have been the closest of friends. The two of them now faced each other with undisguised anger, glaring.
This was wrong... Something was... no, everything felt like a scene from some other world entirely.
Our group -- we had always believed we were the best of friends.
Normally, Mion would be the first to take charge.@k Rena would be the one gently looking after everyone.@k ...But now, the scene before my eyes was completely reversed.
Where exactly had... the gears gone wrong?
Reactions I had never seen from them before... reactions I never imagined possible were churning my heart into chaos, stoking my unease and disappointment to unbearable heights.
...Even so, this was no time to stand still. The situation was dire, and it seemed like it could only get worse from here.
``This is no time to be arguing.@k@vS18/01/160100021. We need to figure out a way to rescue Satoko, no matter what.``
``But how?@k@vS18/03/160300006. We've already failed once.@k@vS18/03/160300007. What can a bunch of kids like us possibly do, Kei-chan?``
``There has to be something even we can do.@k@vS18/01/160100023. If we all put our heads together, I'm sure...``
I said that, but no concrete plan came to mind.
According to Rena, when they appealed to the consultation center last year, they were told that family matters should be resolved within the family... and in the end, it all came to nothing.
As outsiders, there was truly only so much we could do.@k ...In fact, any reckless or self-righteous action might only make things worse for Satoko.
changeReason: Old says 'for us' but context is what they can do FOR Satoko.

TextENGNew: If that was the case... then a method more powerful and effective than appealing to the consultation center, one that could actually help Satoko, would be...
``...That's it!``
An entity more powerful than the consultation center... There was one -- and only one -- force that held Hinamizawa in its grasp and wielded true authority.
Yes... the Sonozaki family!@k If the foremost of the Three Great Families, the ones who united the entire village, brought their power to bear, they could surely find some effective solution...!
changeReason: Old says 'the Three Families leading the village'; JPN says 御三家の筆頭 (the foremost/top of the Three Great Families), meaning specifically the Sonozaki family, not all three. New is accurate.

TextENGNew: ``Mion.@k@vS18/01/160100026. You're the next head of the Sonozaki family, aren't you?@k@vS18/01/160100027. Then ask the people in power to save Satoko!``
``What are you saying, Kei-chan?@k@vS18/03/160300009. Just because I'm the next family head doesn't mean I can do something like that so easily.``
``Why not?@k@vS18/01/160100029. Even government offices jump when the Sonozaki family says the word, right?@k@vS18/01/160100030. Then saving one girl like Satoko should be nothing, shouldn't it?``
changeReason: Old says 'have representatives in government' but 鶴の一声で動く means 'they move at the Sonozaki family's single word.'

TextENGNew: ``...I'm telling you, what's impossible is impossible!@k@vS18/03/160300011. Stop making unreasonable demands!``
It was as if she were pushing me away -- Mion's tone left absolutely no room for argument. Her cold attitude filled me with a sense of wrongness, and beneath that, a rising anger.
changeReason: Old says just 'uncomfortable'; JPN says 違和感 (sense of wrongness) and 不快感 (displeasure/anger). New distinguishes both feelings.

TextENGNew: Impossible...? Unreasonable...? But writing it off from the start isn't going to solve anything either, is it?!
And the Mion I knew would have been the first to take action at a time like this!
``Mion.@k@vS18/01/160100032. Whose side are you on here?!``
``W-whose side...?``

``If you put your mind to it and took action, couldn't you get the Three Families to do something?!@k@vS18/01/160100034. Wasn't Hinamizawa supposed to be a place where everyone fights together to protect one of their own?``
A small groan escaped from Mion as she stood with a severe expression, her head bowed.
...Yeah, I knew. It must be frustrating, having all this dumped on her one-sidedly like this.
But her apparent indifference grated on my nerves and spurred me on.
changeReason: Old says 'submissive attitude' but 我関せずぶり means 'indifference' -- different characterization.

TextENGNew: ``You can't just stand by and watch!@k@vS18/01/160100036. Satoko is being hurt right now!``
changeReason: Old uses 'we'; JPN says ただ黙って見てる (just silently watching), directed at Mion specifically. New's 'You can't just stand by and watch!' is more accurate.

TextENGNew: ``...But last time, Satoko herself refused... she denied everything, remember?@k@vS18/03/160300015. This time will just be the same thing all over again...!``
``Precisely so we don't repeat that mistake, we have to do something.@k@vS18/02/160200026. If things go on like this, Satoko is in real danger...!``
changeReason: Old treats the two clauses as opposed ('can't repeat... but'); JPN 繰り返さないためにも uses ために (in order to), meaning 'precisely to avoid repeating.' New captures the causal link.

TextENGNew: ``Do we have no choice but to convince her? ...Satoko, I mean.``
``Listen, it's not for us to decide.@k@vS18/03/160300017. There are all kinds of people with all kinds of opinions and all kinds of perspectives.``
``Kei-chan, do you grasp how serious this is?@k@vS18/03/160300019. This concerns a person's entire life.``
``You're the one who doesn't understand, Mion!``
changeReason: Old says 'I don't understand you' but Japanese says 'You're the one who doesn't understand' -- completely different.

TextENGNew: ``Then are you fully prepared for the consequences, Keiichi Maebara?!``
changeReason: Old says 'I agree with you' but Japanese 承知の上かよ means 'are you prepared for consequences?' -- opposite meaning.

TextENGNew: Mion suddenly raised her head.
She stared straight into my eyes. With the look of someone facing a bitter enemy...
``Hah! The truth is, you're just enjoying playing the hero, aren't you, Kei-chan?``
``And Rena's the kindhearted fairy godmother?@k@vS18/03/160300023. Ahahahahahahahahahaha!!!``
``How pathetic. You're the real hypocrites here!!!``
``...That's cruel... Mii-chan...``

``Cruel...?@k@vS18/03/160300026. If I'm inhuman, then you two are worse than inhuman!@k@vS18/03/160300027. All you do is spout off one demand after another!@k@vS18/03/160300028. Every time something happens, it's Sonozaki this, Sonozaki that!``

``Are the Sonozakis magicians?!@k@vS18/03/160300030. Are we gods?!@k@vS18/03/160300031. And yet you yourselves just talk -- you have no intention of getting your own hands dirty, do you?@k@vS18/03/160300032. Don't you dare lecture me, you cowards!!``
``You only invoke the Sonozaki name when it's convenient for you!!@k@vS18/03/160300034. I've had enough -- just stop it already!!``
Suddenly, the classroom door was thrown open.
Standing in the doorway was Shion. ...For some reason, her face was pale and haggard, visibly exhausted even at a glance.

``Inhuman, you say...? Hmph, that doesn't even begin to cover it.@k@vS18/06/160600002. ...No, that word isn't enough for this person...@k@vS18/06/160600003. After all... Sis is a 'demon,' right?@k@vS18/06/160600004. From the tips of her toes to the top of her head, down to every last drop of blood!!``
``Sh-... Shion?@k@vS18/01/160100041. What are you doing here...?``
*Slam.*
Shion shoved the door shut with force, then threw a piercing glare at Mion.
``I came to consult with Sis about Satoko.@k@vS18/06/160600006. ...Or so I thought, but it seems that would be a waste of time.``
``You're aware, of course, that Satoko's parents -- the Houjou family -- were supporters of the dam project?``
``Vaguely, but... yeah.``

``At the time, virtually everyone besides the Houjou family was firmly opposed to the dam.@k@vS18/06/160600009. To them, the Houjous were nothing but traitors.@k@vS18/06/160600010. The Sonozaki family especially.@k@vS18/06/160600011. The Houjou family was completely isolated, enemies on all sides.``
``What started as merely cutting ties gradually escalated...@k@vS18/06/160600013. It transformed into outright ostracism and cold treatment.@k@vS18/06/160600014. And that hasn't changed even now.``

``That's why the people of Hinamizawa won't intervene in Houjou family affairs.@k@vS18/06/160600016. Of course, no matter what happens to Satoko... right?@k@vS18/06/160600017. The Sonozaki family takes that stance most strongly, don't they?``
``But the ones who supported the dam were Satoko's parents, weren't they?@k@vS18/01/160100044. Then it's got nothing to do with Satoko!``
But in response to my argument, Shion only gave a pitying smile. And then--
``This is Hinamizawa.``
That single sentence laid bare just how naive and hollow my idealism truly was.
Yes, this was Hinamizawa. Everything I had taken for granted as common sense held no sway here.
A power so immense it could make the entire village sincerely believe that 'crows are white' held dominion over this place.
In other words, unless the Sonozaki family -- seated at the very apex of that power -- gave their approval, nothing could be changed...
``I tried appealing to the Sonozaki main house myself, but... it was completely hopeless.@k@vS18/06/160600019. Dead end all around, basically.``
Defeated words. A hollow laugh from Shion.
Even so, her exhausted expression told me she had done everything she could, fighting as far as she was able to fight.
Shion had been fighting in secret, all this time. Completely alone, never uttering a single complaint to anyone...
I felt ashamed of myself for doing nothing but venting my anxiety, without taking a single real action.
``Did you come all the way here just to say that?@k@vS18/03/160300037. Thanks for the effort, Shion.``
``You've been turning a blind eye to this the whole time, Sis.@k@vS18/06/160600021. That's exactly why I had to come say it myself.``
``Are you done?@k@vS18/03/160300039. Then hurry up and go home, Shion.@k@vS18/03/160300040. There's no seat for you here.``
``You really are a coward, Sis.@k@vS18/06/160600023. You can't say a single thing without Granny's permission.@k@vS18/06/160600024. You're nothing but a gutless loser!``
``Shion!@k@vS18/03/160300042. You have no right to say that to me.@k@vS18/03/160300043. How dare you...!``
Facing Shion's defiant stare, Mion clenched her fists until they creaked.
``Don't you dare say you've forgotten, Shion.@k@vS18/03/160300045. How I've felt all this time... what I've been carrying...@k@vS18/03/160300046. You know, Shion!``
``I wouldn't know about that.@k@vS18/06/160600026. After all, I'm not you, Mion.@k@vS18/06/160600027. I'm the one who was thrown away -- Shion, remember?``
``Shion... you...``

``But this much I can say.@k@vS18/06/160600029. If I were in your place, I would never stay silent.@k@vS18/06/160600030. I would never look the other way while a friend was in danger -- absolutely never!``

``The next family head?@k@vS18/06/160600032. What a joke.@k@vS18/06/160600033. You hold immense influence not just over the Sonozaki family but over all of Hinamizawa, and yet you do nothing...@k@vS18/06/160600034. You're nothing but a figurehead.``
Shion's scathing words left Mion speechless, her head dropping.
...Even standing beside them, I could tell that Shion's accusations had hit home -- and were probably justified.
``What's wrong? Nothing to say? Don't tell me you're crying?``
``Ahahahaha!! How pathetic, Sis!!@k@vS18/06/160600037. So the next head of the great Sonozaki family is just a feeble little crybaby playing dress-up~~?!``
Shion's hollow laughter echoed through the classroom.
...Everyone was overwhelmed by her intensity and sat in stunned silence. She swept her gaze across the room, then spat out her words with evident frustration.

``Right! Right! Of course!@k@vS18/06/160600039. It's none of your business anyway, is it?@k@vS18/06/160600040. So go on pretending to care, playing at being good people!@k@vS18/06/160600041. It's all pretend!@k@vS18/06/160600042. Just an act, right?``
``Go ahead and wring your hands forever!@k@vS18/06/160600044. Just keep chanting 'how sad, how sad' like some empty prayer to make yourselves feel better!!``
Shion flung those words in a raised voice, then turned to leave the classroom as if deciding any further talk was a waste of time. But--
``Ow?! ...K-Kei... chan?``
I had instinctively reached out to stop her, extending my hand toward her shoulder... but she suddenly whirled around, and my hand accidentally struck Shion's cheek.
With the momentum I'd built up, it ended up looking exactly like I'd slapped her.
``That's terrible...``
``Ah, s-sorry...@k@vS18/01/160100046. Are you okay?``
``Taking Sis's side, of all people...!!``
Saying that, Shion took off at a run without another word, as if she refused to hear anything more.
``Wait -- you've got the wrong idea, Shion!``
I hurried after her.
Getting ahead of her, I spread my arms wide to block her path.
Shion clicked her tongue lightly, changed direction in a flash, opened a window, placed her hand on the frame, and vaulted nimbly out into the schoolyard.
``Hey, Shion!``
I leaped out the window after her, chasing her with everything I had. Shion ran desperately, trying to shake me off.
Crossing the schoolyard at terrifying speed, we both barreled toward the school gate.
``Stop following me!!@k@vS18/06/160600049. I don't want anything to do with you, Kei-chan!!``
``Just wait, please!@k@vS18/01/160100050. Just give me a moment -- just listen to what I have to say!``
``There's no point in listening!@k@vS18/06/160600051. You're no different from Sis, Kei-chan!``
``I'm going to save Satoko!@k@vS18/01/160100052. No matter what it takes, I will save her!``
``Anyone can say pretty words.@k@vS18/06/160600053. Who knows what you really feel.@k@vS18/06/160600054. Hah!``
``These aren't just words!@k@vS18/01/160100054. You want to see what's really in my heart? I'll rip it out of my chest right here and show you!``
At that final desperate cry, Shion's pace faltered for just an instant.
Seizing the opportunity, I pumped my arms and legs with everything I had, running for all I was worth.
I somehow managed to close the distance, reached out, and grabbed Shion's right hand. ...That seemed to be the signal she needed to finally stop.
``...Kei-chan, did you... really mean what you just said?``
Shion's voice trembled with ragged breathing. I responded with a deep, silent nod.
``Then... show me.``
``Your heart, Kei-chan... I want to see it.``
I couldn't believe my ears. ...For a moment I thought Shion was joking, but when she turned to face me, her eyes were sharp, her expression cold and mask-like.
``You... are you serious?``
``...Just empty words after all.``
``N-no, that's not it!@k@vS18/01/160100059. I was trying to show you just how serious I am!``
``Every last one of you, nothing but empty talk and hollow promises!@k@vS18/06/160600060. If all you can do is lie, then just shut up and never speak again!!``
Glaring at me with eyes full of loathing, Shion turned on her heel and headed for the school gate.
Her retreating figure radiated unmistakable rejection. I had no words left, no way to stop her -- and just as I was about to give up and watch her go, my gaze fell on 'something' nearby.
...I lacked resolve?
...My words were nothing but lies?
...Was I really going to accept that, Keiichi Maebara...?
Like hell I would...! I was dead serious about Satoko!! I would do whatever it took -- anything!!
Then... in that case...!
``......Hey, Shion.``
I clenched my fist tight, turned toward that 'something' lying on the ground near the storage shed, and broke into a run as if possessed.
Shion turned back, her expression one of utter bewilderment. ...My action had been so impulsive that I hadn't thought about what came next.
I picked up 'that thing' -- a large pair of gardening shears -- drew a breath... and raised the blades to my chest.
The two rusted, dull-looking black blades... caught the sunlight and gleamed with an eerie flash.
``...Kei-chan, what are you doing?``
``Wait... what are you about to do...?@k@vS18/06/160600064. Stop joking around!``
I knew this was insane. But right now, this was the only way I could communicate anything at all.
I closed my eyes and slowly pressed the pointed tip against my chest.
My mind was set, but my body was honest. My knees shook violently, and the skin where the blade touched burned as if on fire.
......The image of Satoko flashed before my eyes.
My fingers tightened around the handle of the shears, strength flooding into my grip.
``Stop this right now!@k@vS18/06/160600066. You don't actually have to do it!@k@vS18/06/160600067. If this is your idea of showing off as a joke, I'm going to be seriously angry!``
``I'm not planning to die.@k@vS18/01/160100062. Like hell I'll die here!@k@vS18/01/160100063. How could I abandon Satoko and die while she's still suffering?!``
``Then why?@k@vS18/06/160600069. This is insane, Kei-chan...``
``You wanted to see my true feelings, right?@k@vS18/01/160100065. A wound like this won't kill me.@k@vS18/01/160100066. I have to save Satoko -- so I'm not going to die!``
I opened my eyes for just a moment. ...Shion stood frozen, staring at me in a mix of shock and disbelief.
``Watch me...``
With those words, I gripped the handle tight and drove the blade deeper with all my strength...
-- But suddenly, the blade stopped dead, as if frozen in place.
When I opened my eyes, Shion had somehow appeared at my side and was gripping the blade of the shears in her bare hand.
``Shion...``
A clear blue sky stretched overhead. Only the sound of our ragged breathing echoed around us.
Before long, Shion lifted her lowered face and looked straight into my eyes.
``...All right...@k@vS18/06/160600073. I believe you... I believe in your feelings for Satoko, Kei-chan...``
``Don't fight alone.@k@vS18/01/160100071. Let me stand with you, Shion.``
She whispered in a cracked voice, then nodded again and again.
``I believe in you, Kei-chan.``
``And I believe in you, Shion.@k@vS18/01/160100073. Let's give it everything we've got, together!``
``Right.@k@vS18/06/160600077. But if you ever pull a theatrical stunt like that again, you won't be forgiven.``
Saying that with a softened expression, Shion delivered a fierce flick to my forehead.
``That's your punishment for scaring me.@k@vS18/06/160600079. Heh heh heh...``
And then we shared a smile. The kind exchanged between comrades pledging themselves to the same cause.
Before I knew it, the shrill cries of the cicadas had given way to the cool, clear song of the higurashi.
Was it encouragement for us, as we readied ourselves to march into battle despite knowing the odds were against us?
Or was it... a mocking laugh, as if to say our struggle was futile...?
